school.title: IEEE CIS Summer School 2026
school.subtitle: Computational Intelligence in Robotics
school.description: Join us for an intensive five-day summer school focusing on the latest advancements in Computational Intelligence applied to Robotics. This program features world-renowned speakers, hands-on workshops, and networking opportunities for PhD students and early-career researchers.

sections.title: About the Summer School
sections.content: The IEEE Computational Intelligence Society (CIS) Summer School 2026 aims to provide participants with a deep understanding of how fuzzy logic, neural networks, and evolutionary computation are transforming the field of robotics. From autonomous navigation to human-robot interaction, we cover the most critical topics in the industry today.

sections.title: Venue & Travel
sections.content: Paderborn is a historic city in Germany, home to one of the leading technical universities. The summer school will be held at the Heinz Nixdorf Institute, a hub for interdisciplinary research in computer science and engineering.

sections.title: Registration
sections.content: Registration is open to graduate students and researchers. IEEE CIS members receive a discounted rate. Applications must include a CV and a short statement of interest.
